g.text: 02 — Install Flow
How does the extension know when a terminal install finishes?
Marketplace UI
[Webview]
installer.ts
[Sets up 3 listeners]
VS Code Terminal
[Runs CLI]
① User clicks 'Install'
② sendText('npx
skills add ...')
Shell Integration
[Detection]
Lock File Watcher
[.skill-lock.json]
30s Timeout
[Safety Net]
Listens for
terminal exit
Watches
.skill-lock.json
30s safety
timeout
First to resolve → cleanup() → dispose listeners → rescan 22 dirs → update TreeView + Webview
Legend
Extension internals
External APIs/services
Filesystem/local
Terminal/CLI
Error paths / safety
Decision annotations
Problem: skills.sh has no install library we
can call from code — only a CLI command
Solution: We run the CLI in a visible VS Code
terminal so the user can see what's happening
Trade-off: Terminal commands are fire-and-forget
— we have no way to know when they finish
Problem: We need to know when the install
finishes, but there's no callback from the terminal
Solution: Race three listeners in parallel:
1. Shell Integration — catches the command exit
2. Lock File Watcher — detects when the install
writes to a known file on disk
3. 30s Timeout — gives up and warns the user
Why three? No single method works everywhere.
Shell Integration needs a newer VS Code. The file
watcher misses symlinks on Windows. The timeout
is a safety net.
Problem: All three listeners could fire for the
same install — we only want to react once
Solution: A simple flag (resolved) lets the first
listener win and shuts down the other two
Result: Progress bar closes → extension rescans
all skill folders → UI updates
